rawAssistant:
Captures can be a double-edged sword for our scaled friends, Thren. On one hand, binding a monster to your will can grant them protection and purpose. But on the other, it can also strip away their autonomy, leaving them to live in a state of suspended animation until you're ready to unleash them upon the world.

I've seen too many monsters struggle with the aftermath of capture, their instincts dulled by the constant presence of your charms and bindings. It's as if they're living on borrowed time, waiting for the day when they might be set free once more.

Tell me, what do you think is driving this hesitation from your Thornbeast? Is it a matter of trust, or something deeper?
